title: FIGS Q3 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: FIGS reported Q3 2021 net revenues of $102.7 million, representing 33.7% year-over-year growth, or 41.4% excluding a $4.2 million related-party sale from prior year. Active customers exceeded 1.7 million, up from 1.1 million year-ago. Average order value increased to $102 from $99 in Q3 2020. Net income was $7 million with diluted EPS of $0.03 (adjusted EPS of $0.05). Gross margin declined one percentage point to 72.7%, primarily driven by enhanced restocking standards and elevated ocean freight rates, partially offset by lower airfreight costs. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 21.6% versus 33.5% in prior year quarter, reflecting public company costs and strategic investments. Management increased airfreight spending to $8-10 million in Q4 to ensure timely delivery of limited-edition products amid global supply chain pressures, representing significant increase from $1 million in Q3 but expected to normalize in 2022. The company raised full-year 2021 guidance to $410 million from $395 million. Long-term targets reaffirmed at $1+ billion revenue by 2025 with 70%+ gross margins and 20%+ adjusted EBITDA margins annually.
